summary: Iteration 9 — NFT Metadata Editor (display_name, description, OpenSea-style traits, lock-on-listing). Backend: 9/9 pytest tests PASS (test_iteration9_nft_metadata.py): owner PATCH success + trim + dedupe (case-insensitive), 403 non-owner, 400 'locked' on open AND cancelled marketplace_listings, admin bypass of lock, 401/403 unauthenticated, 404 missing gen, 400 empty payload, GET /api/generate/{id} returns metadata_locked=False with display_name/description=null and traits=[] defaults. Frontend: critical UI flows verified via Playwright — (1) Locked path on seeded gen 6a3378223bc360a01b1ff640: Studio shows nft-display-name='Stormbreak Test', nft-display-description='Forged in the eye of the tempest.', 2 trait cards (Edition: 1 of 1, Element: Storm); Edit Info button (studio-edit-metadata) visible for admin even on others' creations; modal opens with nft-metadata-locked banner + name/description/save all disabled. (2) Unlocked path on freshly-inserted admin-owned gen: opened modal, banner absent, filled name='UI Test Drop' + description='Forged via Playwright.' + 2 traits + added/removed a third trait via nft-metadata-trait-remove-2, Save fires PATCH, toast 'NFT info saved' appears, modal closes, Studio re-renders with all saved values (display name + description + 2 trait cards Edition/Element).

issues: The CRA dev runtime error overlay catches THREE.GLTFLoader 'Failed to fetch' errors and blocks ALL UI interaction (modals, buttons) until dismissed. Not blocking for real users on production build, but in the preview build it makes the page unusable when a model_url 404s/CORSes. Recommend swallowing the loader error in ModelErrorBoundary so it does not propagate to the dev overlay, OR disabling the dev overlay in this preview env.

action_items: OPTIONAL: Make Studio gracefully recover from a GLB load failure so the rest of the page (Edit Info modal, traits, buttons) stays interactive when the model fails. Currently the CRA error overlay intercepts the THREE error and freezes the whole page until manually closed.

OPTIONAL: Backend PATCH model NFTTrait has Field(min_length=1) on trait_type/value, so a payload with whitespace-only trait_type triggers Pydantic 422 BEFORE the route's manual trim+drop logic runs. The route's 'drop empty trait_type/value' branch is therefore only reachable when trait_type/value contain non-whitespace before strip. This is fine functionally (both reject) but it's inconsistent — consider relaxing the Pydantic min_length to 0 if you want the route's strip-then-drop semantics, or remove the route's drop-empty branch since Pydantic already rejects.
critical_code_review_comments: community_routes.update_generation_metadata — lock check uses find_one on marketplace_listings which scans by generation_id (string). Ensure marketplace_listings.generation_id has an index. If not, this PATCH gets slower as the listing collection grows. Add: db.marketplace_listings.create_index('generation_id').

community_routes.update_generation_metadata — admin bypass is implicit (skips the lock check) but DOES NOT log/audit the bypass. For provenance/trust, admin metadata edits on a listed drop should write to an audit log (e.g. db.admin_audit) so the buyer-facing 'permanent' claim is verifiable. Currently an admin can silently rewrite display_name/traits on a SOLD drop with no trace.

community_routes.update_generation_metadata — `if not update: raise 400 'Nothing to update'` fires when client sends only e.g. {'traits': []}. But cleaned [] is still a valid 'clear all traits' intent. Currently {'traits': []} writes traits=[] (good). But {'display_name': null} sets update['display_name']=None (clears). This asymmetry around clearing vs not-updating is subtle — document it or use a sentinel pattern.

drops_utils.enrich_drop — backfills display_name/description=None but NOT traits=[] (mentioned in request). Verified GET returns traits=null when never set, which is fine since the modal handles null. But the request says 'traits defaults to []'; current behavior is None. Either spec needs to relax to 'None or []' or enrich_drop should default traits to [].

NFTMetadataModal.jsx — locked banner shows when metadata_locked=true, but admin can still bypass on the backend. The frontend gives admin NO UI signal that they are bypassing a lock (Save button is disabled in the modal). Admins viewing a locked drop currently cannot edit via UI at all because the modal disables Save on the client. To realize the 'admin bypass' product rule, the modal should detect role==='admin' and KEEP inputs/Save enabled while still showing the lock banner with an 'Admin override' note. Currently admin-bypass is backend-only and unreachable via UI.

Studio.jsx (~line 687) — Edit Info button visibility is based on ownsCurrent which is `user_id===me.id || role==='admin'`. Correct. But the button is still clickable when metadata_locked=true; clicking shows the modal in fully-disabled state. Consider relabeling the button to 'View NFT Info (locked)' or showing the Lock icon variant (already done — nice).
Backend test infrastructure: register endpoint requires 'name', not 'username'. Update /app/auth_testing.md to clarify.
test_credentials: Admin: admin@bloxdrops.com / BloxDrops2026! (working). Per-run TEST_it9_owner_<hex>@example.com and TEST_it9_other_<hex>@example.com users registered via /api/auth/register {email,password,name}.
seed_data_creation: Per-run mongo inserts: a completed generation owned by the registered TEST owner (cleaned up by fixture teardown), plus transient marketplace_listings documents for the lock tests (deleted in finally blocks). One ad-hoc admin-owned gen (6a34962527fccd2c8bc3bd0f) was inserted then deleted after UI verification. Final DB state: no TEST_it9 leftovers.
context_for_next_testing_agent: NFT Metadata Editor (Phase whatever) verified end-to-end. Seed gen 6a3378223bc360a01b1ff640 currently has display_name='Stormbreak Test', description='Forged in the eye of the tempest.', traits=[Edition:1 of 1, Element:Storm], metadata_locked=true (open or cancelled marketplace listing exists for it) — DO NOT delete those listings or the lock UI test will need new seed data. For unlocked-path UI tests, insert a new gen owned by admin (user_id = str of admin _id 6a33709620f5acbb1a1f7cb3) with status='completed' and a real model_url (use any v3b.fal.media GLB), then visit /studio?view=<gid>. Note: bad model_url causes a CRA dev overlay that blocks the page — always use a real GLB. Auth register uses field name 'name' (NOT 'username').
rca of the issue: No bugs found in the feature under test. Two environment-level frictions encountered: (1) auth_testing docs did not specify 'name' as the register field — quick fix in test fixture; (2) CRA's runtime error overlay catches THREE.js model-load failures from invalid model_urls and blocks the entire page in this preview build — worked around by either using a real GLB url or evaluate-removing the overlay div. Neither affects production users.
